item_a.name: Casper Original Mattress
item_a.image_alt: Casper Original Mattress with three-layer foam construction on white bed frame
pros: Excellent edge support with reinforced perimeter
Balanced feel suitable for multiple sleep positions
cons: May sleep warm for some due to foam construction
Heavier than average at 71 lbs for queen size
Less pressure relief than memory foam competitors
item_a.best_for: Combination sleepers who want a balanced foam mattress with excellent edge support and motion isolation.

item_b.name: Purple Original Mattress
item_b.image_alt: Purple Original Mattress featuring proprietary GelFlex Grid layer on modern platform bed
pros: Proprietary GelFlex Grid provides excellent pressure relief
Superior cooling and airflow compared to traditional foam
Highly responsive surface that adapts instantly
Unique feel that cradles pressure points without sinking
cons: Distinctive feel takes adjustment for some sleepers
Edge support weaker than traditional foam mattresses
Higher price point than comparable foam options
item_b.best_for: Hot sleepers and those seeking maximum pressure relief with a uniquely responsive, cooling sleep surface.

comparison_summary: The Casper vs Purple debate centers on construction philosophy: Casper offers a traditional all-foam design with excellent support and motion isolation, while Purple's innovative GelFlex Grid delivers superior cooling and pressure relief. Casper appeals to those wanting proven comfort at a lower price, while Purple targets hot sleepers and those seeking a uniquely adaptive sleep surface.
verdict_text: Purple wins for hot sleepers and pressure relief seekers, while Casper excels for budget-conscious buyers wanting traditional foam support.

key_differences.item_a: Three-layer polyfoam design (1.5" comfort + 1.5" memory + 7" base)
key_differences.item_b: 2" GelFlex Grid over 3.5" polyfoam base layer

key_differences.item_a: AirScape perforated foam, sleeps neutral to slightly warm
key_differences.item_b: Open-grid design with exceptional airflow, sleeps significantly cooler

key_differences.item_a: Reinforced foam perimeter, excellent for sitting and full surface use
key_differences.item_b: Moderate, grid compresses more at edges

key_differences.item_a: Good with memory foam layer (6.5/10 rating)
key_differences.item_b: Excellent with GelFlex Grid technology (8.5/10 rating)

key_differences.item_a: Excellent foam absorption, minimal partner disturbance
key_differences.item_b: Good but more responsive, slight motion transfer

seo_content:
The Casper vs Purple mattress comparison represents two distinct approaches to modern sleep technology. Casper built its reputation on perfecting traditional foam mattresses with a three-layer design that balances comfort and support, while Purple revolutionized the industry with its proprietary GelFlex Grid that promises pressure relief without heat retention.

When comparing Purple vs Casper construction, the differences are immediately apparent. Casper's Original features 10 inches of carefully engineered polyfoam: a 1.5-inch comfort layer with AirScape perforations, a 1.5-inch memory foam transition layer, and a 7-inch high-density base. This design prioritizes motion isolation and edge support, making it particularly appealing to couples and those who use the entire mattress surface. The reinforced perimeter provides exceptional edge integrity, allowing sleepers to utilize every inch without feeling like they might roll off.

Purple's signature innovation is the 2-inch GelFlex Grid—a hyper-elastic polymer arranged in a column structure that collapses under pressure points while supporting surrounding areas. This creates a floating sensation that's difficult to categorize as soft or firm. Beneath the grid sits a 3.5-inch polyfoam support layer and a 1-inch base, totaling 6.5 inches. While thinner than Casper, the grid's responsiveness creates a supportive feel that belies its modest height.

Temperature regulation strongly favors Purple in the Casper vs Purple debate. The open-grid architecture allows continuous airflow, making it one of the coolest mattresses available. Hot sleepers consistently rate Purple highly for temperature neutrality. Casper's foam layers, despite perforations, retain more body heat—a common limitation of all-foam designs.

Price considerations matter: Casper undercuts Purple by approximately $200 in queen size, offering better value for budget-conscious shoppers. The 100-night trial periods from both companies mitigate purchase risk, though Purple's distinctive feel means some sleepers need the full adjustment period.

For pressure relief, particularly for side sleepers and those with joint pain, Purple's grid technology provides superior contouring around shoulders and hips without the quicksand feeling of memory foam. Casper offers adequate pressure relief but can't match Purple's targeted support. However, Casper's motion isolation surpasses Purple's more responsive surface, making it preferable for couples sensitive to partner movement.

The verdict in this mattress showdown depends on priorities: choose Casper for traditional comfort, excellent edge support, and lower cost; select Purple for maximum cooling, innovative pressure relief, and a uniquely adaptive sleep experience.

faq.q: Which mattress sleeps cooler, Casper or Purple?
faq.a: Purple sleeps significantly cooler due to its GelFlex Grid's open architecture that promotes continuous airflow. Casper's all-foam construction retains more body heat, though its AirScape perforations help somewhat. Hot sleepers should strongly consider Purple.

faq.q: Is Purple worth the extra cost over Casper?
faq.a: Purple costs about $200 more for a queen size. The premium is justified if cooling and pressure relief are priorities, particularly for side sleepers or those with joint pain. Casper offers better value for combination sleepers who prioritize edge support and motion isolation over temperature regulation.

faq.q: Which mattress is better for side sleepers?
faq.a: Purple generally performs better for side sleepers due to its GelFlex Grid providing targeted pressure relief at shoulders and hips. Casper works for lighter side sleepers (under 150 lbs) but may create pressure points for heavier individuals in lateral positions.

faq.q: How long do Casper and Purple mattresses last?
faq.a: Both mattresses typically last 7-10 years with proper care. Purple's hyper-elastic polymer grid is extremely durable and resistant to permanent indentation. Casper's foam layers may show softening around years 6-7. Both companies offer 10-year warranties covering significant sagging.

faq.q: Which mattress has better edge support?
faq.a: Casper significantly outperforms Purple in edge support. Casper's reinforced foam perimeter provides stable sitting edges and full usable surface area. Purple's grid compresses more noticeably at the perimeter, which may bother those who sleep near the edge or sit frequently on the mattress side.

meta_title: Casper vs Purple (2026): Which Mattress Should You Buy?
meta_description: Detailed comparison of Casper vs Purple mattresses. We break down cooling, pressure relief, support, and value to help you pick the right mattress for your sleep style.
